learning_objectives: Understand the theme and moral of the lesson
Develop reading and listening skills
Learn new vocabulary
Identify character traits
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with homework
Students know basic fairy tales and imaginary characters

activities: Ask students whether they like doing homework
Discuss the idea of someone else doing homework
Introduce the character Patrick and the element of magic

methods: Teacher reads the lesson aloud with proper pronunciation
Students take turns reading
Explanation of difficult words and sentences

discussion_points: Introduction to Patrick
The deal between Patrick and the elf
Who actually did Patrick’s homework

class_activities: Match the words with their meanings
Oral one-line answers
Role play between Patrick and the Elf

values_and_moral: Hard work leads to success
Responsibility is important
There are no shortcuts to learning
homework: Write five new words from the lesson with meanings
Write the moral of the story in three to four sentences

lessons.lesson_name: How the Dog Found Himself a New Master!
learning_objectives: Understand the story and sequence of events
Identify characters and their traits
Learn vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and critical thinking
Recognize moral lessons from the story
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with animals and their behavior
Students know simple stories involving animals

activities: Ask students if pets ever make choices about friendship or loyalty
Introduce the story and its setting
Discuss the concept of a dog choosing a master

methods: Teacher reads the story aloud emphasizing character traits
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explanation of difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: Why the dog was unhappy with his free life
The meeting between the dog and the wolf
The decision to serve a master and its consequences

class_activities: Draw the dog and wolf and label their traits
Group discussion on loyalty and choices
Role play the conversation between the dog and the wolf

values_and_moral: Loyalty and trust are important
Consider consequences before making decisions
Understanding and friendship matter in relationships
homework: Write a short paragraph describing the dog’s decision
List three lessons we can learn from the story
evaluation: Participation in role play and discussion

learning_objectives: Understand the story and its sequence of events
Identify the challenges Taro faced and his responses
Learn new vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and critical thinking
Recognize the moral lessons from Taro’s actions
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with family life and household chores
Students know simple stories showing honesty and hard work

activities: Ask students if they have helped their family in household tasks
Introduce the character Taro and his concern for his father
Discuss the idea of working hard for a goal

methods: Teacher reads the story aloud with emphasis on Taro’s emotions
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explanation of difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: Taro’s feelings about his father’s desire for saké
How Taro decides to work harder
The lesson about responsibility and caring for family

class_activities: Group discussion: How would you help your family in a difficult situation?
Vocabulary matching activity
Draw a scene showing Taro helping his father

values_and_moral: Hard work and determination lead to success
Caring for family is important
Responsibility should be taken seriously
homework: Write a short paragraph about Taro’s actions
List two lessons you learned from the story
evaluation: Oral questioning about events and characters
Student participation in discussion and drawing activity

lessons.lesson_name: An Indian-American Woman in Space: Kalpana Chawla
learning_objectives: Understand the life and achievements of Kalpana Chawla
Develop reading and comprehension skills
Learn new vocabulary related to space and science
Recognize qualities like courage, determination, and hard work
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
teaching_learning_materials: Pictures of Kalpana Chawla and space shuttle
previous_knowledge: Students know basic facts about astronauts and space travel
Students are familiar with famous personalities and their achievements

activities: Ask students if they know any famous astronauts
Discuss what qualities are needed to become an astronaut
Introduce Kalpana Chawla and her historic space mission

methods: Teacher reads the passage aloud emphasizing key achievements
Students take turns reading sections
Explain difficult words and technical terms

discussion_points: Kalpana Chawla’s journey to becoming an astronaut
Experiments conducted in space
The challenges and risks of space travel
Importance of education, dedication, and perseverance

class_activities: Group discussion: What qualities make a hero?
Draw a diagram showing Kalpana Chawla in the space shuttle
Write 2-3 sentences describing Kalpana’s achievements

values_and_moral: Hard work and determination can achieve great goals
Courage and perseverance are essential
Learning and curiosity lead to success
homework: Write a paragraph on why Kalpana Chawla is an inspiration
List three qualities you admire in her
evaluation: Oral questioning about facts and achievements
Participation in discussion and drawing activity

learning_objectives: Understand the story and main events
Identify the characteristics of Miss Beam
Learn new vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and critical thinking
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with school life and teachers
Students know about homesickness and new experiences

activities: Ask students about their first day in a new school
Introduce Miss Beam and her qualities
Discuss how teachers can make students feel comfortable

methods: Teacher reads the lesson aloud emphasizing Miss Beam’s description
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explanation of difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: Characteristics of Miss Beam
How her presence comforts students
The teaching methods mentioned

class_activities: Group discussion: How can a teacher make students feel comfortable?
Draw Miss Beam based on the description
Answer questions orally about the text

values_and_moral: Kindness and understanding are important qualities in a teacher
Teachers can help students adapt to new environments
Respect and trust between teachers and students are valuable
homework: Write five new words from the lesson with meanings
Describe Miss Beam in your own words in three sentences
evaluation: Participation in discussion and drawing activity

learning_objectives: Understand the aspirations and dreams of the narrator
Identify the qualities of leadership and responsibility
Learn new vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and critical thinking
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with school and classroom activities
Students understand the concept of dreams and ambitions

activities: Ask students what they want to become when they grow up
Discuss leadership qualities and responsibilities
Introduce the narrator’s ambitions in the lesson

methods: Teacher reads the passage aloud emphasizing key ideas
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explanation of difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: Narrator’s dreams and aspirations
How the narrator plans to make a difference
Qualities of a responsible and visionary person

class_activities: Group discussion: What qualities are important in a leader?
Draw a poster showing the narrator’s dream for India
Oral questions about the narrator’s plans

values_and_moral: Leadership requires responsibility and vision
Helping others and improving society is important
Dreams and goals can inspire action
homework: Write a paragraph about your own ambitions
List three qualities you need to become a good leader
evaluation: Participation in discussion and poster activity

learning_objectives: Understand the story and sequence of events
Identify the main characters and their traits
Learn new vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and critical thinking
Recognize moral lessons about fairness and justice
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with family and social relationships
Students know basic ideas of fairness and justice

activities: Ask students about situations where they felt treated unfairly
Introduce the story and its setting
Discuss the importance of standing up for what is right

methods: Teacher reads the story aloud emphasizing key events
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explain difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: The conflict between the aunt, Jumman, and his wife
How the aunt seeks justice through the panchayat
Algu Chowdhry’s dilemma between friendship and fairness

class_activities: Group discussion: How would you handle an unfair situation?
Role play the conversation between the aunt and Algu
Vocabulary matching activity

values_and_moral: Stand up for what is just and fair
Friendship should not compromise morality
Persistence and courage are important in seeking justice
homework: Write a paragraph on how the aunt fought for justice
List two lessons learned from the story
evaluation: Oral questioning about characters and events
Participation in discussion and role play

learning_objectives: Understand the story and sequence of events
Identify characters and their traits
Learn vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and critical thinking
Understand moral lessons about luck and responsibility
previous_knowledge: Students know the basic concept of games and chance
Students are familiar with decision-making situations

activities: Ask students if they play games of chance
Discuss how luck can affect outcomes
Introduce the story and its main character

methods: Teacher reads the story aloud emphasizing events and characters
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explain difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: The events in the story
How characters respond to luck and chance
Moral lessons about responsibility and decision-making

class_activities: Group discussion: Can we control luck?
Write a paragraph predicting an alternative ending
Vocabulary exercises from the text

values_and_moral: Take responsibility for your actions
Luck alone cannot determine success
Think carefully before making decisions
homework: Write a paragraph on the moral of the story
List key events in the story in sequence

learning_objectives: Understand the features and adaptations of desert animals
Identify the main animals mentioned in the text
Learn scientific vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and observation skills
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
previous_knowledge: Students know basic geography of deserts
Students have knowledge of common animals and their habitats

activities: Ask students about animals they know from deserts
Introduce the topic of desert adaptations
Show pictures of desert animals

methods: Teacher reads the passage aloud emphasizing adaptations
Students take turns reading sections
Explain difficult words and scientific terms

discussion_points: How animals survive in harsh desert conditions
Features that help them adapt to desert life
Importance of studying animal behavior and adaptations

class_activities: Draw a desert animal and label its adaptations
Group discussion: Why is adaptation important for survival?
Match animals with their features

values_and_moral: Understanding nature helps us respect life
Adaptation is key to survival
Observation skills enhance learning about environment
homework: Write a paragraph describing a desert animal
List three adaptations of your chosen animal
evaluation: Oral questioning about animals and adaptations

learning_objectives: Understand the story and its sequence of events
Identify the relationship between humans and nature
Learn new vocabulary in context
Develop reading comprehension and observation skills
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with trees and animals
Students know basic ideas about friendship and curiosity

activities: Ask students about their experiences with animals
Introduce the story and its setting
Discuss friendship between humans and animals

methods: Teacher reads the story aloud emphasizing interactions
Students take turns reading paragraphs
Explain difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: The narrator’s friendship with the squirrel
How trust is built between humans and animals
Moral lessons about care and curiosity

class_activities: Draw the squirrel and narrator with labeled actions
Group discussion on friendship with animals
Answer questions orally from the text

values_and_moral: Friendship and trust are important
Caring for animals teaches responsibility
Observation helps understand nature
homework: Write a paragraph describing the squirrel
List two lessons learned from the story
evaluation: Oral questioning about characters and events

learning_objectives: Understand the meaning of home and family
Learn new vocabulary related to family and relationships
Develop reading and comprehension skills
Express thoughts clearly in writing and speaking
Identify values of love, care, and sharing
previous_knowledge: Students know basic family members and relationships
Students understand the concept of caring for others

activities: Ask students what home means to them
Discuss family relationships and caring
Introduce the poem and its theme

methods: Teacher reads the poem aloud emphasizing rhythm and meaning
Students read lines in turns
Explain difficult words and expressions

discussion_points: Meaning of home beyond a physical place
Examples of love and sharing in the poem
Values highlighted in the poem

class_activities: Write three sentences describing your home
Discuss ways to show care in daily life
Vocabulary exercises from the poem

values_and_moral: Love and care make a house a home
Sharing and kindness strengthen family bonds
Family support is valuable
homework: Write a short paragraph about your family
List five acts of kindness you can do at home

learning_objectives: Understand imagery and descriptive language in the poem
Learn vocabulary related to kites and wind
Develop reading comprehension skills
Identify rhyming and rhythm in poetry
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
teaching_learning_materials: Kite picture or real kite for demonstration
previous_knowledge: Students know what a kite is and how it flies
Students have read simple poems before

activities: Ask students if they have flown kites
Introduce the poem and its descriptive style
Discuss the feeling of flying a kite

methods: Teacher reads the poem aloud emphasizing rhythm and imagery
Students take turns reading lines
Explain difficult words and expressions

discussion_points: How the poet describes the kite’s movements
Imagery and expressions used in the poem
Rhythm and rhyme in the poem

class_activities: Draw a kite and label its movement
Group discussion: How does the kite feel in the wind?
Match words with meanings from the poem

values_and_moral: Observation and description enhance understanding
Creativity is expressed through imagination
Enjoying nature teaches appreciation of beauty
homework: Write five new words from the poem with meanings
Describe a kite flying experience in your own words

learning_objectives: Understand emotions and conflicts in the poem
Learn vocabulary related to disagreements and feelings
Develop reading and comprehension skills
Express ideas and feelings in speaking and writing
Identify cause and effect in conflicts
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with arguments or quarrels with siblings
Students know basic feelings like anger, sadness, and regret

activities: Ask students about small quarrels they have experienced
Introduce the poem and its theme
Discuss how small disagreements can grow

methods: Teacher reads the poem aloud emphasizing emotions
Students read lines in turns
Explain difficult words and expressions

discussion_points: Causes of quarrels in the poem
How the quarrel escalates
Understanding emotions and consequences

class_activities: Role play the quarrel in pairs
Write three sentences about how to resolve conflicts
Vocabulary matching exercise

values_and_moral: Understand the importance of resolving conflicts
Communication is key to resolving misunderstandings
Small quarrels can be avoided with patience
homework: Write a paragraph on a quarrel you resolved peacefully
List three ways to handle disagreements
evaluation: Oral questioning about poem and emotions
Participation in role play and discussion

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of beauty in nature and deeds
Learn new vocabulary related to feelings and actions
Develop reading and comprehension skills
Express ideas clearly in speaking and writing
Identify values and lessons from the poem
previous_knowledge: Students are familiar with nature and good deeds
Students understand basic poetic expressions

activities: Ask students what they find beautiful in nature
Introduce the poem and its message
Discuss how beauty is both external and internal

methods: Teacher reads the poem aloud emphasizing meaning
Students take turns reading lines
Explain difficult words and phrases

discussion_points: Beauty in nature versus inner beauty
Examples from the poem of beauty in actions
Moral and values emphasized by the poet

class_activities: Write 2-3 sentences about something beautiful you saw today
Group discussion: How can we create beauty in our lives?
Vocabulary matching exercises

values_and_moral: True beauty lies in actions and thoughts
Good deeds and happy thoughts create harmony
Appreciation of nature enhances life
homework: Write a paragraph describing inner and outer beauty
List three ways to show beauty in daily life
